accept

Permits an incoming connection attempt on a socket.

SOCKET accept(
  SOCKET s, 
  struct sockaddr FAR *addr,  
  int FAR *addrlen  
);

Parameters

s
[in] Descriptor identifying a socket that has been placed in a listening state with the listen function. The connection is actually made with the socket that is returned by accept.
addr
[out] Optional pointer to a SOCKADDR_IN structure that receives the address of the connecting entity.
addrlen
[in, out] Optional pointer to an integer that specifies the size, in bytes, of the buffer pointed to by addr. When the function returns, addrlen will contain the size of the data copied to addr. If addr is not null, then addrlen must not be NULL.

Return Values

If no error occurs, accept returns a value of type SOCKET that is a descriptor for the new socket. This returned value is a handle for the socket on which the actual connection is made.

Otherwise, a value of INVALID_SOCKET is returned, and a specific error code can be retrieved by calling WSAGetLastError.

The integer referred to by addrlen initially contains the amount of space pointed to by addr. On return it will contain the actual length in bytes of the address returned.

Remarks

The accept function extracts the first connection on the queue of pending connections on socket s. It then creates a new socket and returns a handle to the new socket. The newly created socket is the socket that will handle the actual connection and has the same properties as socket s.

The accept function can block the caller until a connection is present if no pending connections are present on the queue, and the socket is marked as blocking. If the socket is marked as nonblocking and no pending connections are present on the queue, accept returns INVALID_SOCKET with specific error code WSAEWOULDBLOCK. After the successful completion of accept returns a new socket handle, the accepted socket cannot be used to accept more connections. The original socket remains open and listens for new connection requests.

The parameter addr is a result parameter that is filled in with the address of the connecting entity. addrlen is a value-result parameter; it should initially contain the amount of space pointed to by addr; on return it will contain the actual length (in bytes) of the address returned.

The accept function is used with the connection-oriented socket type SOCK_STREAM.

If addr is equal to NULL, then no information about the remote address of the accepted socket is returned. If addr is not equal to NULL, then addrlen must not be NULL, and the integer pointed to by addrlen must be greater than or equal to the size of the SOCKADDR_IN structure.

Error Codes

Error code Meaning
WSANOTINITIALISED A successful WSAStartup call must occur before using this function.
WSAEINPROGRESS A blocking call is in progress.
WSAEINVAL The listen function was not invoked prior to accept.
WSAENOBUFS No buffer space is available.
WSAENOTSOCK The descriptor is not a socket.
WSAEWOULDBLOCK The socket is marked as nonblocking and no connections are present to be accepted.

Requirements

Header: Declared in winsockx.h, for include use Xtl.h.
Import Library: Use Xnet.lib for titles that only support system link play, XOnline.lib for titles that support online play. Use the secure versions of the library file (Xnets.lib or XOnlines.lib) for final testing.

See Also

Winsock Overview, Winsock Functions | bind, connect, listen, select, socket
